req.title: System-Admin views payroll-run
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a payroll-run through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-040 covers three distinct test concerns: (1) a happy-path where an authenticated admin views an existing payroll-run and an audit record is written (EP positive), (2) an unauthenticated request that must return 401 (NEG), (3) a forbidden role (non-admin) that must return 403 (DT decision branch on role), (4) a boundary case where the payroll-run ID is the minimum valid integer boundary value of 1 (BVA), and (5) a state-transition check ensuring the payroll-run module being disabled blocks the view (STC). Tests use pytest with a thin requests-based HTTP client and placeholder fixtures/helpers to stay within the 40-line cap. All tests are idempotent because they only perform read or audit-check operations against a pre-seeded payroll-run fixture.

tests.name: test_admin_views_payroll_run_success
preconditions: System-admin user is authenticated (valid session token)
At least one payroll-run record exists in the system
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <fixture: admin_auth_token>
Retrieve an existing payroll-run id via <helper_fn: get_existing_payroll_run_id>
Send GET /payroll-runs/{id} with Authorization header
Assert HTTP 200 response
Assert response body contains entity id
Assert audit record exists via <helper_fn: assert_audit_record_exists>
expected: Response JSON contains 'id' matching the requested payroll-run id
Audit record written for the view event
trace: R-WEB-040: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_views_payroll_run_success(admin_auth_token, base_url, get_existing_payroll_run_id, assert_audit_record_exists):
    payroll_run_id = get_existing_payroll_run_id()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"}

    response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/payroll-runs/{payroll_run_id}",
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10
    )

    assert response.status_code == 200, (
        f"Expected 200, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(payroll_run_id), (
        f"Entity id mismatch: {body.get('id')} != {payroll_run_id}"
    )
    assert_audit_record_exists(
        entity="payroll-run",
        entity_id=payroll_run_id,
        action="view"
    )

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_payroll_run
preconditions: At least one payroll-run record exists
actions: Retrieve an existing payroll-run id via <helper_fn: get_existing_payroll_run_id>
Send GET /payroll-runs/{id} WITHOUT Authorization header
Assert HTTP 401 response
expected: Response body indicates authentication is required
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_payroll_run(base_url, get_existing_payroll_run_id):
    payroll_run_id = get_existing_payroll_run_id()

    response = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/payroll-runs/{payroll_run_id}",
        timeout=10
    )

    assert response.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}"
    )
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, (
        "Response body should contain an error or message field"
    )

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_viewing_payroll_run
preconditions: A non-admin authenticated user (e.g. role:employee) has a valid token
At least one payroll-run record exists
actions: Obtain a non-admin auth token via <fixture: employee_auth_token>
Retrieve an existing payroll-run id via <helper_fn: get_existing_payroll_run_id>
Send GET /payroll-runs/{id} with the employee Authorization header
Assert HTTP 403 response

tests.name: test_admin_views_payroll_run_with_minimum_boundary_id
preconditions: System-admin user is authenticated (valid session token)
A payroll-run record with id=1 (minimum valid integer id) exists
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <fixture: admin_auth_token>
Set payroll_run_id to 1 (minimum boundary value)
Send GET /payroll-runs/1 with Authorization header
Assert HTTP 200 response
Assert response body id equals 1
expected: No boundary-overflow or validation error returned
trace: R-WEB-040: mandatory fields validated (id at lower boundary)

tests.name: test_admin_cannot_view_payroll_run_when_module_disabled
preconditions: System-admin user is authenticated (valid session token)
Payroll-run module is DISABLED (state transition from enabled to disabled)
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <fixture: admin_auth_token>
Disable the payroll-run module via <helper_fn: disable_payroll_run_module>
Retrieve an existing payroll-run id via <helper_fn: get_existing_payroll_run_id>
Send GET /payroll-runs/{id} with Authorization header
Assert HTTP 403 or 404 response (module disabled)
Re-enable the payroll-run module via <helper_fn: enable_payroll_run_module> in teardown
expected: HTTP status 403 or 404 indicating module is not accessible
Payroll-run data not returned in response body
trace: R-WEB-040: precondition payroll-run module is enabled (inverse state tested)
R-WEB-040: state transition coverage from enabled to disabled
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_admin_cannot_view_payroll_run_when_module_disabled(
    admin_auth_token, base_url,
    get_existing_payroll_run_id,
    disable_payroll_run_module,
    enable_payroll_run_module
):
    disable_payroll_run_module()
    try:
        payroll_run_id = get_existing_payroll_run_id()
        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"}

        response = requests.get(
            f"{base_url}/payroll-runs/{payroll_run_id}",
            headers=headers,
            timeout=10
        )

        assert response.status_code in (403, 404), (
            f"Expected 403 or 404 when module disabled, got {response.status_code}"
        )
        body = response.json()
        assert "id" not in body, "Payroll-run data must not be exposed when module is disabled"
    finally:
        enable_payroll_run_module()

tests.name: test_admin_views_payroll_run_with_invalid_id_returns_400
preconditions: System-admin user is authenticated (valid session token)
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <fixture: admin_auth_token>
Set payroll_run_id to a non-numeric string 'invalid-id'
Send GET /payroll-runs/invalid-id with Authorization header
Assert HTTP 400 response
expected: Response body contains validation error details
trace: R-WEB-040: mandatory fields must be validated
